page: Amazon Web Services mac2-m1ultra.metal is a bare metal macOS Gen2 instance powered by the Apple M1 Ultra chip. This server features 20 physical cores and 20 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz on the arm64_mac architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.4 GB of RAM per core.

The instance does not include local NVMe storage or a discrete GPU, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare metal configuration provides direct access to Apple Silicon hardware, making it a specialized resource density choice for macOS-specific workloads rather than general-purpose x86 computing.

This hardware profile is designed for macOS-based workloads. It is suited for iOS and macOS application development, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, and software compilation tasks that require native Apple Silicon execution.
description: Amazon Web Services mac2-m1ultra.metal is a bare metal macOS Gen2 server powered by an Apple M1 Ultra processor. Utilizing the arm64_mac architecture, this instance delivers 20 dedicated physical cores and 20 vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.4 GB of RAM per core. The server does not feature local storage or a discrete GPU, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Designed specifically for the Apple ecosystem, this bare metal instance offers high resource density for macOS environments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without virtualization overhead. This configuration provides a specialized hardware profile for developers requiring native Apple Silicon execution, making it suited for iOS and macOS application development, native software compilation, and automated CI/CD build pipelines.
